title: Phyllorkis cuprea
intro: Phyllorkis cuprea, also known as the copper orchid, is a plant species belonging to the orchid family, characterized by its unique copper-colored flowers. Phyllorkis cuprea has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis cuprea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are popular remedies for colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Phyllorkis cuprea is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The genus Phyllorkis is further categorized under the tribe Coelogyneae. However, some classifications group it under the subgenus Phyllorkis within the genus Coelogyne. This classification may vary depending on the source and current botanical understanding.
uses: Phyllorkis cuprea is reported to have various medicinal properties, and its uses include treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts are used in traditional medicine to manage pain and reduce swelling. In some cultures, Phyllorkis cuprea is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties make it useful in preventing infections. However, further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and applications.

preparations: Phyllorkis cuprea is a plant species used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations are often employed for their perceived health benefits.
